title: Fluoroscopy Views for Femoral Neck and Intertrochanteric Implant Placement
summary: AP and true lateral (or frog) views to confirm reduction, starting point, and implant position (screws, sliding hip screw, or hemi).
must_know: AP pelvis/hip: assess neck-shaft angle, reduction, screw position relative to calcar and subchondral bone.
Lateral or cross-table: confirm anteversion/retroversion, central screw placement in head, no anterior/posterior penetration.
For intertroch: tip-apex distance (TAD) <25mm on both views for sliding hip screw.
landmarks: Greater trochanter, lesser trochanter, calcar, femoral head center on fluoro.
safe_zones_or_intervals: Screw starting point on AP just below vastus ridge; central on lateral.
danger_zones: Anterior or superior head penetration (high AVN risk); varus reduction.
procedure_specific_relevance: Young femoral neck: anatomic reduction critical; confirm with fluoro before fixation. Avoid malrotation.
Intertroch nail or SHS: TAD and position on both planes to minimize cutout.
common_pimp_questions: What is the target tip-apex distance and why?
Which fluoroscopic views confirm central screw placement in the femoral head?
retrieval_phrases: femoral neck screw fluoroscopy AP lateral TAD
missing_needed_sources: Dedicated OB trauma hip fracture fluoroscopy sections and exact safe starting points/images (current sources focus more on approach and deforming forces than detailed fluoro technique).
notes: Thin module created because v2_1 sources for these procedures emphasize anatomy/deforming forces/approaches more than specific fluoro landmarks. Needs expansion from OB hip trauma pages.

title: Fluoroscopy for Femoral Neck / Intertroch Implant and Reduction
summary: AP and true lateral (cross-table) to confirm reduction, starting point, central head placement, TAD <25mm for SHS.
must_know: AP: neck-shaft angle, calcar reduction, screw position subchondral.
Lateral: anteversion, no AP penetration, central in head.
Intertroch/SHS: TAD on both views to minimize cutout.
Young neck: anatomic reduction before fixation; check rotation.
landmarks: GT, LT, calcar, head center on fluoro.
safe_zones_or_intervals: Start below vastus ridge on AP; central on lateral.
procedure_specific_relevance: Neck: confirm reduction and 3-screw or sliding construct position.
Intertroch: TAD and lag screw position critical.
common_pimp_questions: Which view confirms central head screw?
retrieval_phrases: femoral neck intertroch fluoro AP lateral TAD reduction
missing_needed_sources: Specific OB fluoro sections, exact starting points, and images for neck/intertroch (current sources stronger on approach/deforming forces).
notes: Thin; created because v2_1 hip entries emphasize anatomy more than detailed fluoro technique. Expand from dedicated OB trauma hip pages.
